title: 11 Herbal Creams For Eye Pain
intro_desc: Herbal creams for eye pain can be a natural and effective way to soothe and calm irritated eyes. One reason why herbal teas, when applied topically in cream form, can relieve eye pain is due to their anti-inflammatory properties. For example, Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold, has been used for centuries to reduce swelling and ease pain. It works by blocking the production of chemicals that cause inflammation, which in turn reduces redness and irritation in the eyes.  Aloe barbadensis, or aloe vera, is another herb that's commonly used to treat eye pain. Its gel-like properties can help to cool and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort. Aloe vera also has anti-oxidant properties, which can help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals.  Echinacea purpurea, a type of flowering plant, is often used to boost the immune system, but it can also be used to treat eye pain. When applied topically, echinacea can help to reduce swelling and ease pain, making it an effective remedy for eye irritation.  Using herbal creams for eye pain can bring several benefits to your life. For one, it can help you to avoid using harsh chemicals or artificial products that can irritate the skin and cause more harm. Herbal creams are also generally gentle and non-irritating, making them suitable for people with sensitive skin. Additionally, using herbal creams can be a cost-effective and environmentally friendly way to treat eye pain, as they are often made from natural ingredients that are readily available.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins, particularly calendulin, and sesquiterpene lactones, like dihydroxyacetylsalicylic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The triterpenoids also have antimicrobial properties, making them effective against bacterial infections that may cause eye pain. Additionally, the sesquiterpene lactones have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which help relieve pain and tension in the eyes. By reducing inflammation and soothing the eyes, Calendula officinalis creams can provide relief from eye pain and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for eye pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Dry the Calendula flowers in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) for 2-3 hours.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Calendula flowers to the melted oil and wax mixture. Let it steep for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl. Let it cool and solidify before transferring it to a container for eye cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains a compound called aloin, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. The creams also contain vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which work together to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloin help to reduce swelling and redness, making it easier to alleviate eye pain. Additionally, aloe vera's cooling and hydrating properties help to calm irritated eyes and reduce discomfort. The combination of these properties makes aloe barbadensis creams a suitable option for soothing and relieving eye pain.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for eye pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt the beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat. Add the coconut oil and stir until melted.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for a few minutes. Add the Aloe barbadensis gel and vitamin E oil. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers or jars. Let it cool and solidify completely.
Apply a small amount of the cream to the affected eye area to relieve pain and irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains bioactive constituents like alkylamides, phenylethanoid glycosides, and caffeic acid derivatives. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and protect the delicate tissues around the eyes. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, such as bradykinin and histamine, which contribute to eye pain and irritation. The phenylethanoid glycosides have a soothing effect on the eyes, reducing redness and inflammation, while the caffeic acid derivatives have a potent antioxidant effect, neutralizing free radicals that can exacerbate eye pain. By applying Echinacea purpurea creams to the affected area, you can harness the power of these bioactive compounds to help alleviate eye pain and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for eye pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Echinacea purpurea root powder, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add distilled water to the melted oil mixture and stir until combined.
Add Echinacea purpurea root powder and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir until the powder is fully dissolved.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, use a clean spoon to scoop out a small amount and apply it to the affected eye area as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains active constituents like linalool and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and ease muscle spasms around the eyes, making it effective in alleviating eye pain. The anti-inflammatory effect of rosmarinic acid specifically targets the reduction of inflammation in the eyes, which can be a major contributor to pain. The antispasmodic properties of linalool help to relax the eye muscles, reducing tension and discomfort. By applying Melissa officinalis creams to the affected area, the active constituents can work to soothe and calm the eyes, providing relief from pain.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for eye pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Melissa officinalis flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel.
Steep the dried Melissa officinalis flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes. Let it cool down.
Strain the liquid from step 2 into a bowl and discard the solids. Add the coconut oil and beeswax to the bowl.
Heat the mixture from step 3 in a double boiler until the beeswax melts. Remove from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F.
Add the vitamin E oil and aloe vera gel to the mixture from step 4. Stir well and pour the mixture into a container. Let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavoglycosides, bilobalide, and ginkgolides as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce eye pain and inflammation. Bilobalide has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can calm eye irritation and discomfort. The antioxidant properties of ginkgolides help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and protecting the eyes from damage, Ginkgo biloba creams may help alleviate eye pain and promote eye health.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for eye pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and stir in the Ginkgo biloba extract and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container and let it cool and solidify completely.
Once solid, use a spoon to scoop out small amounts of the Ginkgo biloba cream for eye pain relief as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains gingerol and shogaol, two active constituents that provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds help reduce pain and swelling in the eye by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals. Gingerol also has antioxidant properties that protect the eye from damage caused by free radicals. Shogaol, on the other hand, helps to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which can contribute to eye pain. By applying Zingiber officinale creams to the affected area, the anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties can help alleviate eye pain and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for eye pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup coconut oil, 1/2 cup beeswax, 2 tablespoons Zingiber officinale (ginger) oil, 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl in the microwave.
Once melted, remove from heat and stir in ginger oil and vitamin E oil.
Let the mixture cool and solidify completely. This may take about 30 minutes to an hour.
Once solidified, scoop the cream into a container and use as needed to soothe eye pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains hypericin, a powerful antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and pain. The cream also contains naringenin, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe eye pain. The flavonoids in Hypericum perforatum, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce redness and swelling. These properties make Hypericum perforatum creams a potential solution for eye pain caused by infections, irritations, or injuries. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of the cream can help promote healing and reduce the risk of infection in the affected area.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for eye pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 1/4 cup of shea butter.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of boiling water.
Add 1 tablespoon of Hypericum perforatum (St. John's Wort) oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil to the melted mixture.
Remove the mixture from heat and stir in the shea butter until it's fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar, let it cool, and use as an eye cream for eye pain relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains a key compound called quinine, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain around the eyes. Quinine also has antiseptic properties, which can help prevent infection and promote healing in the affected area. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis creams contain alkaloids such as cinchonine and cinchonidine, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help ease eye pain. These properties work together to reduce eye discomfort and promote a sense of calm. The combination of quinine, cinchonine, and cinchonidine in Cinchona officinalis creams makes it a valuable remedy for relieving eye pain and promoting overall eye health.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for eye pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, 2 cups of water, and a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Steep the dried bark in the water for 2 hours. Then, strain the mixture using the cheesecloth or coffee filter.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil to the strained liquid. Heat the mixture gently until the wax dissolves.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Pour the mixture into a bowl or container.
Let the mixture cool completely, then use a spoon to scoop out a small amount and apply it to the affected eye. Gently massage the cream into the eye area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana creams contains active constituents like tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The tannins in Hamamelis virginiana creams also have a vasoconstrictive effect, which means they can help narrow blood vessels and reduce blood flow to the affected area, thereby relieving eye pain and pressure. Additionally, the flavonoids in the cream have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Hamamelis virginiana creams can provide relief from eye pain and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Hamamelis virginiana herbal creams for eye pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water and 1 cup of witch hazel (Hamamelis virginiana) liquid extract in a saucepan.
Combine 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a separate bowl.
Heat the distilled water and witch hazel mixture over low heat, then add the bowl mixture and stir until the beeswax melts.
Remove from heat and let it cool. Stir occasionally until the mixture thickens and cools down.
Transfer the mixture to a clean container and store it in the fridge. Use a small amount and apply it around the eyes to soothe pain and inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams contains a high concentration of allantoin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce swelling and pain in and around the eyes. Allantoin also promotes cell turnover, which aids in the healing process and speeds up the recovery from eye injuries or irritations. The cream's mucilaginous properties, attributed to its high concentration of mucilage, create a protective barrier on the skin's surface, shielding the eyes from further irritation and allowing them to heal faster. Furthermore, the antiseptic properties of Symphytum officinale help combat infections that can cause eye pain and discomfort. This natural combination of properties makes Symphytum officinale creams a valuable treatment option for soothing eye pain and promoting eye health.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for eye pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Symphytum officinale roots, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the ground Symphytum officinale roots to the melted oil mixture and stir well.
Let the mixture cool and thicken. Pour it into a jar and use it as a cream for eye pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphorbia peplus creams contains the active constituents ingenol mebutate and fagaronine. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce swelling and ease pain. The anti-inflammatory properties of ingenol mebutate and fagaronine help to decrease redness and swelling in the eyes, making it easier to manage eye pain. Additionally, the analgesic properties help to numb the area and reduce discomfort. By reducing inflammation and numbing the area, Euphorbia peplus creams can provide relief from eye pain.
remedies.image_alt: Euphorbia peplus herbal creams for eye pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 250g of Euphorbia peplus leaves and flowers. Clean and dry them completely.
Combine 250g of Euphorbia peplus with 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container. Discard the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the coconut oil mixture. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the wax and butter melt.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store the Euphorbia peplus cream in a cool, dark place.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help with eye pain by reducing inflammation. Some herbal teas, like peppermint or chamomile, contain antioxidants that can calm the eyes and relax the muscles around them. This can make eye pain more manageable, but it might not completely prevent it.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for eye pain daily may not be safe for everyone. Some teas, like chamomile and peppermint, can be a good choice, but others might interact with medications or worsen conditions. If you're experiencing eye pain, start with a small amount and observe how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with eye pain, but it's hard to say exactly how long it takes to feel better. Some people notice a difference within a few hours, while others might need to drink the tea for a few days to feel relief. It really depends on the person and the type of herbal tea they're using.
faq_time: For eye pain, herbal tea can be a soothing relief. Drink it in the evening, before bed, to help calm your eyes. The warmth of the tea can ease tension and promote relaxation. As you fall asleep, your eyes will have a chance to rest and recover from the day's strain.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for eye pain. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and passionflower, have properties that can slow down your blood's ability to clot. If you're taking medications to prevent blood clots or thin your blood, drinking these teas may not be safe. Always be cautious when mixing herbal teas with medications.
faq_pregnant: When it comes to eye pain during pregnancy, some women turn to herbal teas for relief. However, not all herbal teas are safe to consume during pregnancy. Some, like chamomile and peppermint, are generally considered okay, but others like pennyroyal and sage can be too stimulating or cause bleeding.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for eye pain might seem like a soothing solution, but it's not always safe while breastfeeding. Some herbs, like chamomile and lavender, are generally considered safe, but others like comfrey and pennyroyal can pass into breast milk and potentially harm your baby. Always check the ingredients and use caution.
title_2: Eye Pain Relief for Artists: 11 Herbal Creams to Try
